decision-tree.root-question: Is the retrieval domain high-stakes (legal / medical / scientific) where mid-concept splits degrade answers?

branch.question: Does the embedding budget allow one call per sentence at index time?
Is a reliable sentence tokenizer available for the corpus language?

branch.conclusion: Use SemanticChunker with same embedding_model as retriever. Apply r2-threshold-band (default 0.75), r3-size-guards, r5-version-on-threshold-change.
Skip semantic chunking — sentence boundaries are noisy. Fall back to chunking-basics recursive splitter; revisit when spaCy model lands.
Skip. Use chunking-basics recursive splitter; revisit when index-time embedding cost budget grows.
Skip. Use chunking-basics; semantic chunking's index cost is not justified for general prose.

example.p: Civil case corpus, English, budget OK, NLTK punkt reliable → r1-same-model → SemanticChunker with text-embedding-3-small @ threshold 0.75.
